name: Seasons
description: Cycle-based model that uses recurring monthly, weekly, or session tendencies as directional bias and timing context.

context: Recurring monthly, weekly, or session tendency identified.
Current structure aligned with the seasonal bias.
Nearby acceleration and failure levels mapped in advance.
setup: Historical tendency favoring the current directional bias.
Continuation or rotation pattern forming in line with that tendency.
No overriding event has displaced the usual flow.
trigger: Local continuation structure breaking in the cycle direction.
Pullback confirmation printed when the window is early or volatility is mixed.
Remaining expected seasonal range still sufficient after entry.
confirmation: Current session behavior matching the expected seasonal window.
Related market context staying aligned or neutral.
Trend structure remaining orderly with higher lows or lower highs.
invalidation: Current period accepting cleanly against the usual cycle.
Catalyst-driven repricing overriding the historical pattern.
noTrade: Seasonal sample size weak or anecdotal.
Move already extended into major higher-timeframe structure.
Upcoming data event capable of resetting the cycle narrative.

stopLoss: Protective stop outside the local structure that breaks the cycle thesis.
Opposite side of the continuation base or confirming pullback.
Invalidation distance remaining inside planned tolerance.
takeProfit: Primary target at the typical seasonal range extension.
Runner valid only while price structure continues to confirm the cycle.
Profit secured ahead of known reversal windows when the pattern usually fades.
timeExit: Position closed when the seasonal window ends.
Exposure reduced before the next macro event that can reset regime.
Hold period contained within the edge-bearing cycle window.
earlyExit: Current cycle diverging sharply from expected flow.
Structure breaking against the thesis before target progression.
Correlated market context flipping hard against the idea.
